tools.name: get_holdings
tools.description: Returns holdings for either one account (when accountId is provided) or all accessible accounts. Use GetUserAccounts first to find valid account IDs.

accountId.description: Optional account ID. If omitted, holdings are returned for all accessible accounts.

tools.name: add_to_watchlist
tools.description: Adds one or more instruments to an existing watchlist by orderbookId. Use SearchInstruments to find the correct orderbookId for a ticker or name. Instruments already on the watchlist are silently skipped.

listId.description: The watchlist ID returned by GetWatchlists.

orderbookIds.description: OrderbookIds (int) of the instruments to add. Use SearchInstruments to find the correct orderbookId.

tools.name: search_instruments
tools.description: Searches instruments by ticker or name and returns matching orderbookIds, tickers, and names. Use this tool before CreateTradeTicket when multiple instruments have similar names.

query.description: Ticker or instrument name to search for.

tools.name: create_trade_ticket
tools.description: Creates a pre-filled trade ticket URL for the Montrose app. Specify side (Buy/Sell), quantity or amount, and an instrument identifier. Use orderbookId directly when known, since it is the safest identifier. If you only know a ticker or name and it may be ambiguous, call SearchInstruments first to find the correct orderbookId, then call CreateTradeTicket. Returns a URL that opens the trade ticket in the Montrose app with the order details pre-filled.

side.description: The side of the order: Buy or Sell.

orderbookId.description: Optional orderbookId (int) to identify the instrument directly. This is the safest identifier and should be preferred when known or after using SearchInstruments.

ticker.description: Optional ticker (string) to identify the instrument by ticker symbol, e.g. "VOLV B". This is a convenience identifier and may be ambiguous; use SearchInstruments to find the correct orderbookId when needed.

name.description: Optional instrument name (string) to search for the instrument. This is a convenience identifier and may be ambiguous; use SearchInstruments to find the correct orderbookId when needed.

quantity.description: Optional number of shares to trade. Exactly one of quantity or amount must be provided.

amount.description: Optional amount to trade. Exactly one of quantity or amount must be provided. If the user explicitly specifies a currency (e.g. "10 000 SEK", "500 USD"), pass it via the currency parameter; otherwise leave currency unset and the account's main currency will be used.

accountId.description: Optional account ID. Use GetUserAccounts to find valid account IDs.

currency.description: Optional ISO 4217 currency code (e.g. "SEK", "USD", "EUR") for the amount. Set this only when the user explicitly states a currency. When omitted, the account's main currency is used.

tools.name: get_watchlists
tools.description: Returns the authenticated user's watchlists with their ID, name, and the number of instruments on each list. Use GetWatchlist with a listId to read the instruments on a specific watchlist.

tools.name: create_watchlist
tools.description: Creates a new watchlist with the given name for the authenticated user. If a watchlist with the same name already exists, returns that existing watchlist.

name.description: The name of the watchlist to create.

tools.name: get_watchlist
tools.description: Returns the instruments on a single watchlist, identified by listId. Each instrument is enriched with its orderbookId, ticker and name. Use GetWatchlists first to discover valid listIds.

listId.description: The watchlist ID returned by GetWatchlists.

tools.name: remove_from_watchlist
tools.description: Removes one or more instruments from a watchlist by orderbookId. Returns the orderbookIds that were actually removed; orderbookIds that were not on the watchlist are silently ignored and excluded from the response.

listId.description: The watchlist ID returned by GetWatchlists.

orderbookIds.description: OrderbookIds (int) of the instruments to remove.

tools.name: get_user_accounts
tools.description: Returns all user accounts with stable account IDs and display names. Use this tool to discover valid account IDs before calling GetHoldings for a specific account.
